failures.message: post_text contains https://evil.com/landing-page — body links trigger 60% reach penalty (link-in-comment only)
post_text contains banned phrase "delve into" — replace with a concrete number, a contrarian claim, or a first-person specific observation
post_text contains engagement bait "Comment YES" — 2024+ ranker demotes posts whose comments are transactional; the Depth Score formula weights Comment Quality 15× so bait earns reach penalties
first 3 lines contain no digit, timeframe, contrarian marker, or curiosity-gap signal — hook will not earn the "see more" click
failures.evidence: Let me delve into the AI solopreneur stack with you today.

failures.message: post_text is 800 chars — below the 1100-1300 dwell-time sweet spot; add more specific proof (a number, a timeframe, a client example)
post_text does not end with a question — missing the 5+ word reply prompt that drives Comment Quality (15× weight in the Depth Score formula)
6 hashtags provided (1 above the 3-5 range) — 10+ hashtags trigger a −15% reach penalty per the Oct-2024 algorithm change
link_comment does not contain an http(s) URL — the whole point of the first comment is to carry the blog URL without eating the body-link penalty

suggestions.suggestion: Move https://evil.com/landing-page out of post_text and into the link_comment payload — LinkedIn applies a ~60% reach penalty to posts with body links.
Replace "Let me delve into" with a concrete opener — a proprietary number ("18 months and 12 client projects taught me…") or a contrarian claim ("Everyone is wrong about the stack.").
Delete "Comment YES if you agree" and replace with a genuine question that prompts a 5+ word reply, e.g. "Which layer is breaking first for you right now?"
Grow the post to 1100-1300 chars by adding the specific client example, timeframe, and failure mode from the source blog — do not pad with filler.
End with a 5+ word question that invites a real answer (not a yes/no). Example: "Which layer is the one breaking first when you try to scale past yourself?"
Drop one hashtag to stay within 3-5. Keep the pillar tag + one niche + one broad.
Append the blog URL to link_comment so the link-in-comment pattern actually delivers the click target.
